# roadmap: dependency-hard, priority-soft execution order + ready/blocked_by
mkdir proj
cd proj
exec mtt init --template hierarchy
stdout 'initialized'

# an epic + three tasks with priorities
exec mtt add --type epic 'E'
stdout 'created e1'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 --priority low 'setup'
stdout 'created t1'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 --priority high 'feature'
stdout 'created t2'
exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 --priority medium 'docs'
stdout 'created t3'

# t2 (high) depends on t1 (low): the low blocker must precede its high dependent
exec mtt dep add t2 t1

# human roadmap: two axes + propagation.
exec mtt roadmap
# hard dep: t1 (low) precedes t2 (high)
stdout '(?s)t1  \[low\].*t2  \[high\]'
# PROPAGATION: t1 (low) is lifted ABOVE t3 (medium) — it inherits t2's high, so a
# low task outranks an independent medium one (impossible under own-priority order)
stdout '(?s)t1  \[low\].*t3  \[medium\]'
# t2 carries the depends_on annotation
stdout '↳ blocked by: t1'
# the epic e1 sinks to the end (its children precede it) and lists them
stdout '(?s)t3  \[medium\].*e1  \(tbd\)  E'
stdout '↳ contains: t\d, t\d, t\d'

# --json: non-null arrays + honest priority
exec mtt roadmap --json
stdout '"id": "t1"'
stdout '"priority": "high"'
stdout '"priority": ""'
stdout '"ready": true'
stdout '"blocked_by": \[\]'
# t2's blocked_by lists t1
stdout '(?s)"blocked_by": \[\s*"t1"\s*\]'
# a leaf's contains is [] (non-null); the epic's lists its children
stdout '"contains": \[\]'
stdout '(?s)"contains": \[\s*"t\d"'

# list --sort priority puts the (unique) high task first
exec mtt list --sort priority
stdout '(?s)t2  task  \[tbd\]  feature.*t1  task  \[tbd\]  setup'

# an invalid --sort is rejected with the extended error text
! exec mtt list --sort bogus
stderr 'want created\|updated\|priority'

# an invalid --priority on add is a usage error
! exec mtt add --type task --parent e1 --priority urgent 'x'
stderr 'invalid --priority'

# show prints the priority
exec mtt show t2
stdout 'priority: high'
